title: Custom-keyword extension point (x-jit-*)
description:
A registration point for `x-jit-*` custom JSON Schema keywords, plus a regression test guarding graceful degradation.

## Background

Future declarative cross-field logic will be added as custom keywords rather than a second formalism. Research confirmed the `jsonschema` crate treats unknown keywords as annotations (no strict-reject mode), so schemas using `x-jit-*` degrade gracefully under a standard validator; this task guards that property against future bumps. See decision record sections 5.3 and 11.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] The engine exposes a registration point that registers `x-jit-*` custom keywords via `jsonschema::ValidationOptions::with_keyword` and the `Keyword` trait.
- [ ] [hard] A regression test confirms a schema using an `x-jit-*` keyword still validates structurally under a standard (no-custom-keyword) validator, i.e. unknown keywords are annotations, not errors.
- [ ] [aspirational] A sample keyword (optionally CEL-backed) is demonstrated end-to-end.

## Notes

Low priority, off the critical path. Degradation is confirmed (jsonschema `compiler.rs`); the test exists to catch a regression on a future crate bump.
